content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 ShiftForce LLC: Full-Stack Developer — .NET + React + Azure — Remote (US) Anywhere in the World Missouri Full-Stack Programming Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5210/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Kansas City <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.shiftforce.com">https://www.shiftforce.com</a> </p> <p>**About us**</p> <p>ShiftForce is scheduling and operations software for restaurants. We've been around a while, we're profitable, and we ship real features to real users every week. Small team — there are no layers of management between you and the work, which means real ownership and real impact, but also means everyone pitches in on whatever needs doing.</p> <p>**The role**</p> <p>We need a full-stack developer who works like an owner. You'll pick up problems from the backlog, ask the questions you need answered, then build and ship the solution. We're not looking for someone who needs every decision made for them — but we're also not looking for someone who treats every ticket as an excuse to rewrite a subsystem.</p> <p>A typical week might be shipping a couple of features, fixing a few bugs, debugging a weird Azure Function timeout, reviewing a teammate's PR, and pushing back on a product idea that doesn't make sense. Some weeks lean more toward infra and ops — a broken pipeline, a slow query. Because we're small, you'll wear many hats. You should be comfortable solving technical problems that aren't strictly "writing code," and you shouldn't expect a dedicated DevOps or DBA team to hand you a clean environment.</p> <p>**Stack**</p> <p>- Backend: C# / .NET Core 10, some legacy .NET Framework (being migrated)<br>- Frontend: React, Next.js, TypeScript, CSS<br>- Mobile: React Native / Expo<br>- Data: SQL Server, PostgreSQL — real queries, real indexes, real execution plans<br>- Infra: Azure Functions, App Service, DevOps pipelines, Key Vault</p> <p>**You're a fit if you**</p> <p>- Have 3+ years shipping production code on a similar stack<br>- Solve problems instead of escalating them — you read the logs, write a repro, check the docs<br>- Build what was asked for, not what you wish was asked for<br>- Are comfortable wearing many hats: code, infra, debugging, customer issues, whatever the week needs<br>- Use AI coding tools (Cursor, Claude, Copilot) as part of your daily workflow — not as a novelty<br>- Communicate clearly in writing (most of our communication is async)</p> <p>**Logistics**</p> <p>- 100% remote, US-based<br>- Full-time<br>- Slight preference for candidates in Las Vegas or Kansas City — not a requirement</p> <p>**How to apply**</p> <p>Email justinb@shiftforce.com with a short note describing a hard bug you debugged recently — what was broken, how you found it, how you fixed it. That's the application. Resume optional.</p> <p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/shiftforce-llc-full-stack-developer-net-react-azure-remote-us">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/shiftforce-llc-full-stack-developer-net-react-azure-remote-us</a></p> Wed, 20 May 2026 14:08:40 +0000 Fri, 19 Jun 2026 14:08:40 +0000 https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/shiftforce-llc-full-stack-developer-net-react-azure-remote-us https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/shiftforce-llc-full-stack-developer-net-react-azure-remote-us Orchard Brokerage: Jr Appointment Setter Anywhere in the World Texas Sales and Marketing Contract <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> New York, New York <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://orchard.com/">https://orchard.com/</a> </p> <p>Quick read before you apply.</p> <p>This job is simple.</p> <p>You call people.</p> <p>You find out if they have a house. You find out if they want to sell it.</p> <p>That's it.</p> <p>If they say yes, you book the appointment with our closer.</p> <p>Then you move on to the next one.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <hr> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>What You Don't Do</h2> <p>You don't pitch.</p> <p>You don't sell.</p> <p>You don't talk about price or commission.</p> <p>That's not your job.</p> <p>Your job is to find people who want to talk.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <hr> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>What A Day Looks Like</h2> <p>You dial for 4 hours.</p> <p>You talk to about 30 people.</p> <p>Out of those, 3-5 will raise their hand.</p> <p>Out of those, 1 will end up on our closer's calendar.</p> <p>That's a good day.</p> <p>You log the call. One line. Move on.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <hr> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>The Pay</h2> <p>$4-5/hr base. Paid Twice a month.</p> <p>Bonuses on top:</p> <ul> <li> <p>$5 every time our closer gets on the phone with someone you passed over</p> </li> <li> <p>Bumps to $8 per call once you hit your weekly quota</p> </li> <li> <p>$5 every time our closer books the appointment</p> </li> <li> <p>$25 every time we get the listing</p> </li> <li> <p>$2,500 cash when 12 deals close from your work — plus all your bonuses double for the next year</p> </li> </ul> <p>The top people on our team make way more than their base.</p> <p>The math works if you consistently do the reps.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <hr> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>The Schedule</h2> <p>20 hours a week. Part-time.</p> <p>Fully remote.</p> <p>You pick your hours. Just be on during US business hours (8am-7pm, CST US time zone).</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <hr> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>Who We Want</h2> <ul> <li> <p>You're comfortable on the phone</p> </li> <li> <p>Rejection doesn't bother you</p> </li> <li> <p>You follow a script. You don't freelance.</p> </li> <li> <p>You're organized. You can track 50 calls a day without losing your place.</p> </li> <li> <p>Your English is strong enough that people trust you on the phone</p> </li> <li> <p>You want to get paid for results</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <hr> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>Who We Don't Want</h2> <ul> <li> <p>People who want to "sell"</p> </li> <li> <p>People who think a script is beneath them</p> </li> <li> <p>People who go quiet after 3 "no"s in a row</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <hr> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>How to Apply</h2> <p>Send us two things:</p> <p>1. Your resume.</p> <p>2. A audio recording of you reading this script out loud.</p> <p>This is the actual script you'd run on the job. It's called the Expired Script and we use it when we call homeowners whose listing just came off the mark
